name: Adidas
description: German sportswear giant, world's second-largest after Nike

name: Airbus
description: European aircraft manufacturer, Boeing's main rival, also defense & space

name: Allianz
description: Europe's largest insurer, also asset management (PIMCO)

name: BASF
description: World's largest chemical company, German industrial cornerstone

name: Bayer
description: German pharma & crop science group, acquired Monsanto, Roundup litigation

name: Beiersdorf
description: German skincare company — Nivea, Eucerin, La Prairie brands

name: BMW
description: German premium automaker — BMW, Mini, Rolls-Royce cars

name: Commerzbank
description: Germany's second-largest commercial bank, corporate & SME focus

name: Continental
description: German auto parts & tire manufacturer, autonomous driving tech

name: Covestro
description: German specialty chemicals, polycarbonates & polyurethanes, ex-Bayer

name: Deutsche Bank
description: Germany's largest bank, investment banking & asset management

name: Deutsche Börse
description: Operator of Frankfurt Stock Exchange, Eurex derivatives, Clearstream

name: DHL Group
description: World's largest logistics company (ex-Deutsche Post), express, freight, supply chain

name: Deutsche Telekom
description: Europe's largest telecom by revenue, majority owner of T-Mobile US

name: E.ON
description: Major German utility, electricity & gas distribution, energy networks

name: Fresenius
description: German healthcare group — hospitals, dialysis (Fresenius Medical Care), nutrition

name: Hannover Re
description: World's third-largest reinsurer, property/casualty & life/health

name: HeidelbergMaterials
description: Major global cement & building materials producer (ex-HeidelbergCement)

name: Infineon Technologies
description: German semiconductor maker, leader in automotive chips & power semiconductors

name: Mercedes-Benz
description: German luxury automaker, trucks & vans, EV transition

name: Merck KGaA
description: German pharma & life science group (not US Merck), lab equipment & materials

name: Munich Re
description: World's largest reinsurer, also primary insurance (ERGO)

name: Porsche SE
description: Holding company of Porsche/Piëch families, controls Volkswagen Group

name: Porsche AG
description: German luxury sports car maker (911, Cayenne, Taycan), VW subsidiary

name: Qiagen
description: Dutch-German biotech, molecular diagnostics & sample preparation tech

name: Rheinmetall
description: German defense & automotive supplier, tanks (Leopard), ammunition, military vehicles

name: RWE
description: Major German utility, largest wind power producer in Europe, energy transition

name: SAP
description: Europe's largest software company, enterprise ERP & cloud business solutions

name: Sartorius
description: German bioprocess equipment & lab instruments, pharma manufacturing tools

name: Siemens
description: German industrial conglomerate — automation, trains, smart infrastructure

name: Siemens Energy
description: German energy technology — gas turbines, wind (Gamesa), grid solutions

name: Siemens Healthineers
description: German medical technology — imaging (MRI, CT), lab diagnostics, Varian oncology

name: Symrise
description: German flavors & fragrances producer, food/cosmetics ingredients

name: Volkswagen
description: World's largest automaker by revenue — VW, Audi, Porsche, Lamborghini, Skoda

name: Vonovia
description: Europe's largest residential real estate group, ~550k apartments in Germany

name: Zalando
description: Europe's largest online fashion platform, German e-commerce

name: Brenntag
description: World's largest chemical distributor, middleman between producers & industries

name: MTU Aero Engines
description: German aircraft engine manufacturer, maintenance & repair (MRO)

name: Delivery Hero
description: German food delivery platform, operates in 70+ countries (not in Germany itself)

name: HelloFresh
description: World's largest meal kit delivery company, German-founded
